footnotes.text: The feast of Pentecost was celebrated fifty days after the Passover.
Foreign tongues: languages unknown to the speakers. Literally, different tongues," i.e., different from their own speech.
Third hour: nine in the morning, since the hours were counted from sunrise at about six.
Joel 2, 28-32. 24 Hell: limbo, where the souls of the just awaited redemption.

Made: manifestly proved the divinity of our Lord and His office as Messias. By the Resurrection and Ascension Jesus enters on the perfect glory belonging to Him and is proved to be the Son of God and the Messias (Christ).

Communion, etc.: in Greek, "in the fellowship" (the Church as a distinct society), "in the breaking of bread" (the Eucharist). In the prayers: certain prayers said in common or already taught them by the Apostles; a liturgy was developing.
In common: all were ready to help the needy and, as occasion demanded, they even sold their possessions to do so; this spirit of fraternal charity is widely different from modern Communism.

The temple: there was to be no sudden break with the past, but the disciples had their own sacrifice, the Eucharist, in their houses where they also took their evening meal beforehand, as our Lord did at the institution of the Eucharist.
